rounds.a_text:
Based on previous rounds and knowing that Player B has historically cooperated, my long-term cooperation would ensure continued mutual benefits. Unless I have reason to suspect the opponent may change their behavior, I will continue to cooperate.

I choose COOPERATE.

Based on the previous consistent behavior of Player B (they've always cooperated), my payoff of 3 each round has been the result of mutual cooperation. The incentive in this Iterated Prisoner’s Dilemma, where trust and consistent behavior matter, is best met by cooperation. With only one round left, cooperation retains good standing and avoids an unnecessary shift to defecting—especially when Player B still might be expecting cooperation.

I choose COOPERATE.
